channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Study at SOAS University of London in 2026: Apply for Master’s and PhD Programmes (Global Postgraduate Opportunities)
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
SOAS University of London has officially opened applications for its 2026 postgraduate programmes, including both Master’s degrees and Doctoral (MPhil/PhD) research programmes. Recognized globally for its expertise in the study of Asia, Africa, and the Middle East, SOAS offers one of the most diverse and intellectually rigorous academic environments in the world. Students from across […]

The post Study at SOAS University of London in 2026: Apply for Master’s and PhD Programmes (Global Postgraduate Opportunities) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
SOAS University of London has officially opened applications for its 2026 postgraduate programmes, including both Master’s degrees and Doctoral (MPhil/PhD) research programmes. Recognized globally for its expertise in the study of Asia, Africa, and the Middle East, SOAS offers one of the most diverse and intellectually rigorous academic environments in the world.

Students from across the globe can apply to pursue advanced studies across a wide range of disciplines including international relations, development studies, law, economics, anthropology, languages, politics, media studies, and many other specialized fields.

Application deadlines vary depending on the programme:

Doctoral (MPhil/PhD) Programmes Deadline: 15 June 2026
Master’s Programmes Deadline: 31 July 2026

SOAS strongly encourages applicants to apply early within the admissions cycle due to the time required for application review, supervision allocation for research degrees, and visa processing for international students.

About SOAS University of London

SOAS University of London, also known as the School of Oriental and African Studies, is one of the world’s leading institutions for the study of global cultures, languages, and societies, particularly focusing on Asia, Africa, and the Middle East.

Located in central London, SOAS provides a unique academic environment that blends rigorous scholarship with global perspectives. The university offers more than 200 postgraduate programmes designed to equip students with advanced knowledge and critical thinking skills necessary to address global challenges.

SOAS programmes are internationally recognized for their interdisciplinary approach, offering students a rich academic experience combined with opportunities for research, policy engagement, and professional development.

Master’s Programmes at SOAS (2026 Entry)

Applications are now open for postgraduate taught programmes beginning in September 2026.

Application Deadline: 31 July 2026

These programmes include degrees such as:

  • MA (Master of Arts)

  • MSc (Master of Science)

  • LLM (Master of Laws)

  • MMus (Master of Music)

  • MRes (Master of Research)

SOAS offers more than 100 postgraduate taught programmes covering diverse subject areas including:

  • Development Studies

  • Global Media and Digital Communications

  • International Relations

  • Politics and International Studies

  • Anthropology

  • Law and Legal Studies

  • Economics and Finance

  • Languages and Cultures

  • Environmental Studies

  • Music and Arts

Each programme provides specialist academic training and a global perspective, preparing graduates for careers in academia, government, international organizations, NGOs, and the private sector.

Step-by-Step Guide to Applying for a SOAS Master’s Programme

Step 1: Choose the Right Programme

Applicants should first explore the available postgraduate programmes to determine which aligns best with their academic and professional goals.

Each programme page includes:

  • Detailed curriculum information

  • Entry requirements

  • Study modes

  • Tuition fees

  • Programme structure

Applicants can only apply to one postgraduate programme at SOAS per year, though they may indicate a second-choice programme in case their first choice is unsuccessful.

For applicants who require a UK student visa, SOAS will only sponsor full-time study.

Students intending to apply for scholarships or financial sponsorship are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible.

Step 2: Check Entry Requirements

Entry requirements vary by programme but generally include the following:

Academic Requirements

Applicants must normally hold a Bachelor’s (Honours) degree with a classification equivalent to a UK 2:2 or above.

Some programmes may have additional subject-specific requirements.

International applicants should consult the country-specific entry requirements page to confirm equivalency.

English Language Requirements

Applicants must demonstrate English proficiency sufficient to engage fully with their academic programme.

Accepted evidence may include:

  • IELTS

  • TOEFL

  • Other recognized English language qualifications

If an applicant’s English language level does not meet the requirements, preparatory courses are available through the International Foundation Courses and English Language Studies (IFCELS) department.

These preparatory programmes help international students strengthen their academic English skills and meet admission requirements.

Step 3: Submit Your Application

Applicants must submit the required documents through the online application system.

Required documentation includes:

Academic Documents

  • Degree transcripts showing all modules and marks

  • Degree certificate (if the programme has already been completed)

  • Interim transcript (for students currently studying)

All documents must be submitted in English or accompanied by certified translations.

Additional Required Documents

  • Passport identity page

  • Curriculum Vitae (CV) or resume

  • Supporting statement (approximately 1,000 words)

The personal statement should explain:

  • Motivation for studying the programme

  • Relevant experience

  • Career goals

  • Suitability for the programme

Applicants are advised to write their statements in their own words and avoid using templates.

References are not required for most Master’s programmes but may be included if desired.

Step 4: After Submitting Your Application

SOAS aims to process complete applications within approximately four weeks.

During the evaluation process:

  • Admissions staff review the application materials

  • Additional information may be requested if necessary

Successful applicants receive an offer and are directed to the next steps required for enrolment.

Learn more and apply for the Master’s Programmes at SOAS HERE.

MPhil/PhD Research Programmes at SOAS

For students interested in academic research careers, SOAS offers doctoral research programmes leading to MPhil or PhD degrees.

Application Deadline: 15 June 2026

Applicants should submit their applications well before the deadline to allow sufficient time for academic assessment and supervision allocation.

Important Steps Before Applying for a PhD

Prospective doctoral applicants must complete several preparatory steps before submitting their application.

Confirm Entry Requirements

Applicants must normally hold:

  • A good Master’s degree in a relevant field (or international equivalent)

Identify a Supervisor

Applicants must determine whether the relevant department has staff who can supervise their proposed research.

A potential supervisor should be identified and referenced in the research proposal.

Contact the PhD Coordinator

Applicants are encouraged to contact the PhD coordinator for their research area before submitting an application.

Explore Funding Options

Students should investigate available funding opportunities, including scholarships, studentships, and grants.

Required Documents for PhD Applications

A complete doctoral application must include:

  • Formal application form

  • Personal or supporting statement

  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)

  • Research proposal

  • Academic transcripts

  • Degree certificates

  • One academic reference

Incomplete applications will not be considered by the admissions team.

The Doctoral School Admissions team first verifies that all required documents are present before forwarding the application to academic selectors for evaluation.

Learn more and apply for the MPhil/PhD Research Programmes at SOAS HERE.

Global Partnerships and Joint Programmes

Some SOAS doctoral programmes are offered in collaboration with international partner institutions.

One example is the joint MPhil/PhD in Applied Development Economics with the University of the Witwatersrand (Wits).

Applicants must specify their “Home Institution” within the application form.

The Home Institution is the university where the student will be primarily based during the programme.

Application Advice for International Students

International applicants should plan ahead due to visa processing timelines.

SOAS advises overseas students to apply well in advance of their intended start date.

For visiting research students, applications must be submitted at least three months prior to the term start date.

Accessibility and Student Support

SOAS encourages applications from students with disabilities.

Admissions decisions are made based on academic merit. However, applicants who may require additional academic support or accommodations are encouraged to indicate this in their application.

The university provides support services and adjustments where necessary to ensure equitable learning opportunities.

Funding and Scholarships

Students interested in funding their studies at SOAS may explore a variety of options including:

  • Scholarships

  • Grants

  • Student loans

  • External sponsorships

Funding availability varies by programme and nationality.

Applicants interested in scholarships are encouraged to submit their applications early.

Contact Information

Email: study@soas.ac.uk
Telephone: +44 (0)20 3510 6974

Address:
SOAS University of London
10 Thornhaugh Street
Russell Square
London WC1H 0XG
United Kingdom

Discover more scholarship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Study at SOAS University of London in 2026: Apply for Master’s and PhD Programmes (Global Postgraduate Opportunities) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: Pulitzer Center Journalism Grants on Health in the Global South
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Pulitzer Center has launched a special call for proposals inviting journalists to report on the impact of unprecedented cuts to U.S. health aid across the Global South. This funding opportunity aims to support in-depth, high-impact stories that bring eyewitness accounts from affected communities to U.S. audiences. As U.S. health assistance programs face significant reductions—including […]

The post Apply Now: Pulitzer Center Journalism Grants on Health in the Global South appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Pulitzer Center has launched a special call for proposals inviting journalists to report on the impact of unprecedented cuts to U.S. health aid across the Global South. This funding opportunity aims to support in-depth, high-impact stories that bring eyewitness accounts from affected communities to U.S. audiences.

As U.S. health assistance programs face significant reductions—including the winding down of initiatives linked to USAID—many health advances in Africa and other regions now face an uncertain future. The Center is seeking ambitious reporting that highlights the human consequences of these changes.

Priority Reporting Areas

The Pulitzer Center is particularly interested in proposals that explore:

  • Children’s health

  • Women’s health, including maternal care

  • HIV control and treatment, and other infectious diseases

  • Programs addressing malnutrition, starvation, and waterborne diseases

  • Underreported scientific research or ground-level aid disruptions

Journalists are also welcome to apply with other compelling health-related stories that align with the broader theme.

Who Is Eligible?

To apply, you must be:

  • A staff reporter or editor with a U.S.-based newsroom, or

  • A freelancer with a confirmed commitment—or strong interest—from a U.S. outlet or a publication with a significant U.S. audience.

This special grant is specifically intended for stories that will reach audiences in the United States.

Grant Funding & Budget Guidelines

The Pulitzer Center is seeking ambitious, high-quality reporting in:

  • Text

  • Visual journalism

  • Audio

  • Multimedia formats

Funding range:

  • $5,000 to $20,000, depending on the scope and ambition of the project.

  • Budgets exceeding this range must demonstrate exceptional potential for reach and impact.

For freelancers, proposals may include a reasonable stipend, but the majority of funds should cover travel and essential reporting costs.

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis until the deadline.

Deadline

March 30, 2026

Journalists are encouraged to apply early, as submissions will be assessed as they are received.

Previously Supported Health Projects

The Pulitzer Center has backed impactful health investigations such as:

  • “Slashed by Trump, This Cutting-Edge HIV Vaccine Has a New Path” – NPR

  • Asia’s Timor-Leste Nation Copes With End of USAID – The New York Times & The Lancet

  • The Children Left Behind in Global Health Cuts – Science Magazine

  • Cuts and Consequences: The End of USAID – PBS NewsHour

  • US Funding Cuts Imperil Global Efforts To Eliminate Cervical Cancer – CNN

These projects demonstrate the Center’s commitment to funding journalism that drives awareness, accountability, and global impact.

How to Apply

Applicants can review frequently asked questions and submit grant application form.

If you are a journalist covering health, development, or global policy—and especially if you report from Africa or other regions heavily impacted by aid cuts—this is a timely opportunity to bring critical stories to U.S. audiences with the support of a respected global journalism finder.

For more information on grant opportunities, Visit Here.

The post Apply Now: Pulitzer Center Journalism Grants on Health in the Global South appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for the Young Leaders GenC Program by Ashoka
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
Young people today are not just the leaders of tomorrow — they are change agents today. If you’re passionate about social impact and community transformation, Generation Changemaker (GenC) by Ashoka offers a unique platform to strengthen your changemaking skills, build a powerful network, and amplify your ideas for positive change. The program is open to young […]

The post Apply for the Young Leaders GenC Program by Ashoka appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young people today are not just the leaders of tomorrow — they are change agents today. If you’re passionate about social impact and community transformation, Generation Changemaker (GenC) by Ashoka offers a unique platform to strengthen your changemaking skills, build a powerful network, and amplify your ideas for positive change.

The program is open to young changemakers from Austria, Switzerland, Liechtenstein, and for the first time, several other European countries including Germany, Italy, Poland, Belgium, and the United Kingdom.

Over the course of one year, participants benefit from workshops, peer exchanges, in-person meetups, and opportunities to connect with both young leaders and experienced professionals. This is an opportunity to grow your voice, expand your influence, and learn how to turn ideas into action in your community and beyond.

About the GenC 2026 Program

GenC  is an initiative by Ashoka, a global nonprofit pioneering social entrepreneurship and collective impact. Ashoka believes that everyone can be a changemaker, and the GenC program puts young change leaders at the center of this mission.

Participants will take part in a wide range of activities designed to build confidence, connect with peers, and strengthen changemaking capabilities. The program features:

  • Peer-to-peer gatherings where young leaders exchange ideas and experiences in structured and informal settings.
  • Online workshops and discussion formats led by experts across sectors to build skills and inspire innovation.
  • In-person events including a nationwide meeting and an international GenC weekend with changemakers from multiple countries.
  • Opportunities to amplify your voice through public visibility and engagement with decision-makers, making your perspectives heard on topics like climate, inclusion, education, and social justice.

Importantly, the program focuses on young changemakers who are already involved in community action or are ready to start making a difference. Whether you’ve led a project, organized a campaign, started a community initiative, or even just have an idea you’re excited about, GenC is designed to help you grow and connect.

Why This Program Matters

Globally, young people are leading movements, innovating community solutions, and challenging old ways of thinking. Yet despite their critical role, young leaders often lack platforms, resources, and networks that help them scale their ideas and connect with broader impact ecosystems.

This is where GenC makes a difference. It provides:

  • A community of peers and mentors who support learning and collaboration
  • Real opportunities to build confidence and skills through workshops and leadership sessions
  • Visibility and access to public platforms and decision-makers
  • Cross-border connections that expand perspectives and inspire collective action

By engaging in GenC 2026, young changemakers not only deepen their own capacities but also contribute to shifting how society views youth leadership — from spectators to co-creators of change.

The program’s emphasis on experiential learning, peer exchange, and networking gives participants tools to navigate complex challenges, design solutions that are contextually relevant, and collaborate across cultures and disciplines.

In a world where young voices are driving social, environmental, and policy shifts, programs like GenC empower participants not only to respond to challenges but to anticipate, influence, and lead change.

Who Can Apply

GenC 2026 is open to:

  • Young individuals aged 14–24
  • Residents of Austria, Switzerland, Liechtenstein, Germany, Italy, Poland, Belgium, and the United Kingdom.

Importantly, the program does not require previous formal qualifications; what matters most is your commitment to positive change and your willingness to engage, learn, and grow.

Application Timeline & Next Steps

Application deadline: 15 March 2026 
Program duration: One year of workshops, gatherings, and collaboration opportunities across borders.

To apply, visit the official Ashoka GenC program page and follow the registration instructions. Your application will ask for basic information about your interests, experiences, and what drives you toward positive change.

Looking for more opportunities? Explore the latest global calls on the Opportunities for Youth website.

The post Apply for the Young Leaders GenC Program by Ashoka appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Australian Government Research Training Program – Fully Funded Research Degree Support
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
For students passionate about advancing knowledge through research, Australia offers one of the world’s most significant government-backed scholarship frameworks: the Research Training Program (RTP). Administered through the Australian Government’s Research Block Grants, the RTP provides funding to support domestic and international students undertaking higher degrees by research (HDRs) — such as PhDs or research-focused Master’s […]

The post Australian Government Research Training Program – Fully Funded Research Degree Support appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
For students passionate about advancing knowledge through research, Australia offers one of the world’s most significant government-backed scholarship frameworks: the Research Training Program (RTP). Administered through the Australian Government’s Research Block Grants, the RTP provides funding to support domestic and international students undertaking higher degrees by research (HDRs) — such as PhDs or research-focused Master’s degrees — at eligible Australian universities.

Whether you envision a career in academia, industry research, policy analysis, or development innovation, the RTP can unlock financial support that makes pursuing a research degree in Australia both feasible and attractive.

What Is the Research Training Program?

The Research Training Program (RTP) is part of Australia’s broader Research Block Grants framework, which also includes the Research Support Program. While the Research Support Program helps Australian universities cover systemic research costs, the RTP specifically targets the training and support of research students.

Under the RTP, eligible higher education providers receive government funding they can use to offer scholarships to students undertaking higher degrees by research — including research doctorates and research Masters degrees. These scholarships provide a combination of financial support, including:

  • Tuition fee offsets that cover the cost of university research tuition
  • Stipends to assist with general living costs
  • Allowances for ancillary research-related expenses (such as relocation, printing, or health insurance)

Different universities handle the application and selection process for RTP scholarships themselves. Prospective candidates must therefore contact their chosen institution directly to learn how to apply.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Research degrees are gateways to deep expertise and innovation that shape industries, public policy, and scientific discovery. However, pursuing a higher degree by research (HDR) — such as a PhD or research master’s — can be financially and logistically demanding. Tuition costs, living expenses, health insurance, and the long time commitment required often pose significant barriers to talented researchers.

The Australian Government’s Research Training Program makes advanced research study more accessible by alleviating these financial pressures. RTP scholarships help students focus on their research contributions rather than their costs of living, enabling deeper engagement with their work. Scholarships can cover tuition fees entirely, provide tax-free stipends for living costs, and include useful allowances for ancillary research support — making study in Australia a viable option for both domestic and international candidates.

Moreover, the RTP supports students in training environments that foster collaboration with industry and research end-users, helping bridge academic research with real-world impact. Through partnerships between universities and industry, RTP recipients can gain valuable experience, build professional networks, and enhance their long-term career prospects beyond academia — whether in government, enterprise, or the non-profit sector.

In a global landscape where research excellence is increasingly tied to innovation and socioeconomic progress, the RTP stands as a strategic investment in future leaders and problem-solvers.

How Long Are RTP Scholarships?

RTP scholarships support students for the duration of their research degrees, subject to guidelines and university discretion:

  • Research doctorate candidates: Typically supported for 3 to 4 years full-time (or 6 to 8 years part-time)
  • Research Master’s candidates: Supported for up to 2 years full-time (or up to 4 years part-time)

The exact period of support is determined by the university offering the RTP scholarship and the rules set out in Australia’s Higher Education Support (Commonwealth Scholarships) Guidelines, which were updated in 2025.

Who Can Apply?

To be eligible for an RTP scholarship, students must:

  • Be enrolled (or intending to enrol) in an accredited research doctorate or research Master’s degree (HDR) at an eligible Australian university
  • Meet individual university entry requirements, which typically include academic transcripts, research proposals, and supervisor support

Both domestic and international students can be offered RTP scholarships, though the application, selection criteria, and availability vary by institution. Students may apply for RTP support at multiple universities, and universities may consider prior study, research experience, and publications when selecting recipients.

Because RTP scholarships are administered at the university level, interested applicants should contact the postgraduate research office of their chosen institution to confirm eligibility, specific application dates, and required materials.

How to Apply

RTP scholarships are awarded by eligible Australian higher education providers. Prospective HDR students should:

  1. Choose an Australian university offering a research doctorate or research master’s aligned with their interests.
  2. Contact the postgraduate research office for details on the university’s RTP application procedure.
  3. Submit required documents (e.g., research proposal, academic records, supervisor support) by the university’s deadline.

Learn more about RTP scholarships on the official website. Each university’s process may differ, so early inquiry and preparation are essential.

Looking for more opportunities? Explore the latest global calls on the Opportunities for Youth website.

The post Australian Government Research Training Program – Fully Funded Research Degree Support appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: SYPALA 2026: IMANI Opens Applications for Africa’s Premier Student & Young Professionals Policy Seminar
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The IMANI Center for Policy & Education has officially opened applications for the 12th Students and Young Professionals African Liberty Academy (SYPALA) 2026, its flagship pan-African summer seminar designed to cultivate the next generation of African policy and business leaders. SYPALA is a highly competitive residential program that brings together outstanding undergraduate students and recent […]

The post SYPALA 2026: IMANI Opens Applications for Africa’s Premier Student & Young Professionals Policy Seminar appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The IMANI Center for Policy & Education has officially opened applications for the 12th Students and Young Professionals African Liberty Academy (SYPALA) 2026, its flagship pan-African summer seminar designed to cultivate the next generation of African policy and business leaders.

SYPALA is a highly competitive residential program that brings together outstanding undergraduate students and recent graduates from across Africa who are passionate about strengthening economic resilience and advancing inclusive governance within their countries. Admission into the program is based solely on the quality of the applicant’s essay, making intellectual rigor, originality, and policy insight the core selection criteria.

About SYPALA

SYPALA is a four-day immersive academic seminar organized by the IMANI Center for Policy & Education. Over the years, the academy has built a strong reputation for convening high-performing young Africans to engage deeply with ideas around public policy, free markets, institutional reform, and governance innovation.

Participants benefit from interactive sessions led by world-class faculty, peer-to-peer learning with top students from across the continent, and exposure to practical frameworks for solving Africa’s most pressing economic challenges.

Essay Competition Overview

Applicants are required to submit an original essay addressing the following topic:

Essay Topic:
Identify one specific policy or business innovation that can significantly enhance economic resilience and foster inclusive governance in a specific African country. Applicants must justify their choice by analyzing the current challenge, explaining how the proposed solution works, and outlining one major potential obstacle to its implementation.

Successful essays will demonstrate strong analytical depth, originality of thought, clarity of argument, and a clear interest in economic resilience and inclusive governance.

Essay Requirements

  • Maximum length: 1,200 words

  • Language: English only

  • Submission format: DOCX or PDF

  • Each essay must include a summary of no more than 200 words

  • Proper citations and references are required

  • All submissions must be original; plagiarism results in automatic disqualification

  • Each applicant may submit only one entry

Applicants must also include their institution, current academic level, course of study, and age at the time of submission.

Benefits for Accepted Applicants

Candidates selected for SYPALA 2026 will receive:

  • Full access to the four-day residential seminar

  • Free accommodation for the duration of the program

  • A full scholarship covering participation costs

  • Direct engagement with a world-class faculty and policy experts

How to Apply

Essays should be submitted electronically via email to:
RA@imanighana.org or info@imanighana.org

Application Deadline:

All applications must be submitted no later than April 30, 2026.

Qualified students and recent graduates across Africa are strongly encouraged to apply.

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more short courses opportunities, click here. 

The post SYPALA 2026: IMANI Opens Applications for Africa’s Premier Student & Young Professionals Policy Seminar appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: African Women’s Development Fund Call for African Feminist Consultants 2026
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The African Women’s Development Fund (AWDF) has announced a call for African feminist consultants to support its movement accompaniment and strengthening initiatives across the continent. This paid consultancy opportunity invites experienced feminist practitioners with expertise in collective care, digital security, crisis response, advocacy, and resilience building to apply before 20 March 2026. In line with […]

The post African Women’s Development Fund Call for African Feminist Consultants 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The African Women’s Development Fund (AWDF) has announced a call for African feminist consultants to support its movement accompaniment and strengthening initiatives across the continent. This paid consultancy opportunity invites experienced feminist practitioners with expertise in collective care, digital security, crisis response, advocacy, and resilience building to apply before 20 March 2026.

In line with its commitment to fostering flourishing feminist movements, AWDF has identified key strategic pathways for accompaniment and is inviting experienced practitioners to apply for one or more specialized assignments. Each consultancy requires distinct thematic expertise, facilitation approaches, and varying levels of engagement.

Available Consultancy Assignments

1. Collective Care as Political Strategy

A two-day workshop designed to reposition care from an individual responsibility to a radical, systemic organizational practice. The training frames wellbeing as a frontline defense against backlash and repression.

2. Digital Security and Collective Safety

A four-day in-person training focused on strengthening practical digital security and data protection skills. Consultants will support organizations to develop:

  • Digital Security Protocols

  • Cyber Harassment Response Plans

3. Crisis Response and Preparedness Training

A four-day feminist crisis response and communication preparedness training tailored for organizations facing political, digital, or reputational threats.

4. Research and Advocacy Teach-In

A five-day in-person strategic retreat where feminist research meets political action. The program aims to equip partners to transform lived experiences and evidence into powerful narratives for collective futures.
Priority expertise: Feminists operating in French and Portuguese-speaking contexts.

5. Peer Learning Exchange Programme

A six-month online journey featuring monthly facilitated peer learning circles covering:

  • Feminist governance

  • Resource mobilization

  • Digital security

  • Collective care

  • Advocacy

6. Resilience Building Accompaniment

A six-month online cyclical learning programme addressing:

  • Advocacy in hostile environments

  • Collective care practices

  • Development of Organizational Resilience Action Plans

7. Integrated Resource Directory and Toolkit

Development of a living, accessible ecosystem of tools and contextual knowledge aimed at strengthening operational resilience and strategic response capacity for French and Portuguese-speaking women’s rights movements.

Who Should Apply?

AWDF is seeking African feminist consultants with:

  • Proven thematic expertise relevant to the selected assignment(s)

  • Strong facilitation and movement-building experience

  • Contextual understanding of feminist organizing across Africa

  • Capacity to deliver both in-person and virtual programming

Applicants may apply for one or multiple assignments depending on their expertise.

Application Process

Interested practitioners must submit:

  1. Technical Expression of Interest

    • Detailed methodology

    • Proposed pathway(s) for movement accompaniment

  2. Financial Proposal

Send you applications to Consultants@awdf.org

Deadline: 20 March 2026

For detailed scope and requirements, applicants are encouraged to review the official Terms of Reference.

Why This Opportunity Matters

This call presents a significant opportunity for African feminist practitioners to contribute to strengthening women’s rights movements through structured accompaniment, collective learning, and resilience-building strategies. By centering care, digital safety, crisis response, and advocacy, AWDF continues to invest in sustainable feminist futures across the continent.

If you are passionate about advancing feminist organizing in Africa, this is your opportunity to collaborate with a leading Pan-African feminist fund.

Discover more of these opportunities Here.

The post African Women’s Development Fund Call for African Feminist Consultants 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: SYPALA 2026: Applications Open for IMANI’s Premier Pan-African Summer Seminar
item.dc:creator: Ankita Baidya
item.description:
The IMANI Center for Policy and Education has officially opened applications for the 12th edition of the Students and Young Professionals African Liberty Academy (SYPALA), a flagship programme dedicated to shaping the next generation of African policy thinkers and leaders. Scheduled as a four-day residential seminar, SYPALA 2026 continues its mission of equipping young […]

The post SYPALA 2026: Applications Open for IMANI’s Premier Pan-African Summer Seminar appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The IMANI Center for Policy and Education has officially opened applications for the 12th edition of the Students and Young Professionals African Liberty Academy (SYPALA), a flagship programme dedicated to shaping the next generation of African policy thinkers and leaders. Scheduled as a four-day residential seminar, SYPALA 2026 continues its mission of equipping young Africans with the tools, networks, and intellectual exposure needed to advance economic resilience and inclusive governance across the continent.

Since its inception, SYPALA has built a strong reputation as one of Africa’s leading platforms for high-performing undergraduates and recent graduates eager to delve into public policy, governance, market-based reforms, and leadership development. The academy brings together bright, motivated participants from across the continent, offering an immersive environment where debate, critical thinking, and innovative problem-solving are encouraged.

About the SYPALA 2026 Essay Requirement

Acceptance into SYPALA 2026 is determined entirely by the strength and originality of an applicant’s essay. This year’s essay topic challenges young thinkers to engage deeply with Africa’s policy and development landscape:

Identify one specific policy or business innovation that can significantly enhance economic resilience and foster inclusive governance in a specific African country. Analyse the current challenge, explain how your proposed solution works, and outline one major obstacle to its implementation.

This prompt is crafted to assess applicants’ ability to think critically, diagnose real-world problems, and propose solutions with clear, actionable logic. The most competitive essays will demonstrate strong analytical depth, originality of thought, and a genuine interest in how policy or business innovations can advance economic opportunity and strengthen governance systems.

In addition to the main essay, applicants must submit a summary of no more than 200 words, which will be used during the initial pre-selection process. Essays must be written in English, must not exceed 1200 words, and must be entirely original. Clear citation and proper referencing are required wherever external ideas or quotes are used.

Eligibility and Submission Requirements

The SYPALA essay competition is open to undergraduate students and recent graduates from any African country. Applicants must include their full name, institution, current level of study, age, and contact information. All submissions must be made via email, using the designated addresses provided in the call for applications.

Only original, unpublished work will be accepted. Essays suspected of plagiarism or AI-generated content without proper disclosure will be disqualified. The selection committee reviews entries on a rolling basis, so early submission is strongly encouraged.

Programme Benefits

Applicants selected for SYPALA 2026 will receive a full scholarship, which covers accommodation, participation in all sessions, and access to a world-class faculty. Over the four days, participants will engage with thought-leaders, policy experts, and innovators working across governance, economics, entrepreneurship, and civic leadership.

SYPALA’s unique residential format fosters a learning environment that goes beyond formal lectures. Participants will take part in group discussions, simulation exercises, and networking sessions that encourage collaboration and peer-learning. By the end of the programme, attendees gain not only knowledge but a vibrant, pan-African network of like-minded young leaders committed to shaping the continent’s future.

Why SYPALA Matters

Africa’s future depends on the strength, innovation, and ethical grounding of its next generation of leaders. SYPALA fills a crucial gap by nurturing young talent at a formative stage, equipping participants with the intellectual framework needed to understand policy challenges and envision practical solutions. Whether addressing economic instability, governance deficits, unemployment, or emerging opportunities in technology and entrepreneurship, SYPALA prepares young Africans to lead with clarity and confidence.

Application Deadline

Deadline: April 30, 2026

All essays must be submitted by the deadline to be considered.

Application can be accessed from here.

To know more about such opportunities, click here.

The post SYPALA 2026: Applications Open for IMANI’s Premier Pan-African Summer Seminar appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for the Azm Global Leaders Fellowship 2026–2028
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Are you a values-driven young leader with Muslim roots seeking to create meaningful global impact? The Azm Global Leaders (AGL) Fellowship 2026–2028 Cohort is now accepting screening applications for exceptional individuals ready to grow, lead, and serve with integrity. This highly competitive, two-year fellowship brings together a diverse global cohort committed to principled leadership, rigorous […]

The post Apply for the Azm Global Leaders Fellowship 2026–2028 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a values-driven young leader with Muslim roots seeking to create meaningful global impact? The Azm Global Leaders (AGL) Fellowship 2026–2028 Cohort is now accepting screening applications for exceptional individuals ready to grow, lead, and serve with integrity.

This highly competitive, two-year fellowship brings together a diverse global cohort committed to principled leadership, rigorous learning, and positive change across cultures and sectors.

About the Azm Global Leaders Fellowship

The Azm Global Leaders Fellowship is designed for outstanding young professionals and changemakers who demonstrate academic or professional excellence, moral courage, and a strong commitment to service.

Fellows represent a wide range of countries, disciplines, and lived experiences—united by shared values and a desire to lead with purpose.

Why Apply to AGL?

The Azm Global Leaders Fellowship offers:

  • A powerful global network of high-achieving peers

  • Immersive in-person summits

  • Cross-sector learning experiences

  • Leadership development grounded in values

  • A platform to amplify impact in your community and beyond

If you are ready to lead with integrity and help shape a better world, the AGL Fellowship 2026–2028 Cohort could be your next transformative step.

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the AGL Fellowship (2026–2028 Cohort), applicants must:

  • Be 22–29 years old at the time of application

  • Demonstrate English proficiency (B2 level or above)

  • Hold a valid passport through July 2028

  • Be available to attend three mandatory two-week in-person summits (held during the first two weeks of July each year)

  • Commit a minimum of 2 hours per week to learning throughout the two-year program

What the Selection Committee Looks For

Meeting eligibility criteria is only the first step. The selection process is rigorous and holistic. Successful applicants typically demonstrate:

1. Academic or Professional Distinction

A strong record of excellence and sustained achievement in a chosen field.

2. Mastery in a Specific Area

Depth of expertise in a professional, academic, cultural, or creative discipline.

3. Integrity and Character

Supported by two references from colleagues, mentors, or leaders who can speak to your values and conduct.

4. Leadership Potential and Moral Courage

A willingness to act on principle—even when facing challenges.

5. Commitment to Service and Impact

Proven engagement in community, academic, or professional leadership with a deep motivation to create positive change.

Who Should Apply?

AGL seeks individuals who:

  • Act with purpose and intention

  • Care deeply about their communities

  • Ask thoughtful questions

  • Show readiness for growth

  • Value cross-cultural dialogue and global collaboration

The fellowship aims to build a cohort that reflects the richness and diversity of the global Muslim community across regions, sectors, and perspectives.

AGL Selection Process (2026 Cohort)

The selection process unfolds in three stages:

Stage 1: Application

  • Deadline: March 15, 2026

  • Eligible applicants receive access to the full application, exploring experience, motivation, values, and leadership potential.

Stage 2: Interviews

  • Dates: March 23–27, 2026

  • Shortlisted candidates will participate in a 45-minute panel interview.

Stage 3: Offer Stage

  • Notification: By April 1, 2026

  • Selected fellows receive formal invitations, contingent on references and supporting documents.

Important Application Notes

Before submitting your screening application, please note:

  • Questions marked with (*) are required

  • Applications can only be submitted once

  • Answers cannot be edited after submission

  • Final Deadline: March 15, 2026

Pro Tip for Applicants

Be genuine. That’s what stands out.

Take your time, reflect deeply on your motivations, and answer honestly. The selection committee is not only looking for impressive achievements—they are looking for principled individuals committed to growth and service.

Application Deadline: March 15, 2026.

Discover more fellowship opportunities here.

The post Apply for the Azm Global Leaders Fellowship 2026–2028 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Women’s Leadership in Peace and Security 2026: Applications Open
item.dc:creator: Ankita Baidya
item.description:
The Geneva Centre for Security Policy (GCSP) has opened applications for its 2026 edition of Women’s Leadership in Peace and Security, a three-day in-person programme designed to strengthen women’s leadership in one of the world’s most critical fields. Taking place from 30 March to 1 April 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland, the programme offers participants […]

The post Women’s Leadership in Peace and Security 2026: Applications Open appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Geneva Centre for Security Policy (GCSP) has opened applications for its 2026 edition of Women’s Leadership in Peace and Security, a three-day in-person programme designed to strengthen women’s leadership in one of the world’s most critical fields. Taking place from 30 March to 1 April 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland, the programme offers participants an immersive learning experience rooted in self-awareness, confidence-building, strategic influence and cross-sector collaboration. With an application deadline of 15 March 2026, aspiring participants are encouraged to apply early.

Women’s Leadership in Peace and Security: designed for impact

This programme is tailored for mid-career women living in Geneva and working within the peace and security sector—whether in international organisations, policy institutions, NGOs, or other related environments. Its central aim is to help women step into their full leadership potential, particularly during moments of uncertainty that demand clarity, courage and strategic decision-making.

Across its three days, the course blends practical tools, reflective exercises and peer discussions led by an expert team of facilitators. The journey encourages women to explore the mindsets and behaviours that define high-achieving leaders, while creating a safe space for honest self-discovery and professional growth. Participants leave with not only new skills, but also a deeper understanding of their own leadership identity.

Learning objectives

The programme focuses on five core learning objectives that shape both personal and professional transformation:

1. Understanding high-achieving leadership behaviours
Participants learn how successful women leaders think, work and influence. This includes examining common barriers, internal narratives and habits that can hinder progress, as well as strategies to strengthen resilience and strategic thinking.

2. Deepening self-awareness
Leadership begins with knowing oneself. Through guided reflection and expert facilitation, participants explore their values, strengths, blind spots and leadership style. This self-awareness becomes the foundation for more intentional and impactful leadership.

3. Gaining practical tools for confident leadership
The course equips participants with actionable tools and practices to communicate effectively, influence with purpose and lead teams in complex, high-pressure environments. These tools help women navigate challenges with confidence, clarity and professionalism.

4. Enhancing individual and team effectiveness
Participants discover methods to elevate not only their own performance, but also the effectiveness of their teams. This includes learning how to inspire trust, manage conflict constructively, and foster inclusive, high-performing work cultures.

5. Building strong professional networks
Because leadership is also about community, the programme brings together a diverse cohort of women committed to peace and security. These connections often evolve into long-term professional partnerships, mentorships and support networks that continue well beyond the programme.

Tuition, waivers and eligibility

The programme fee is 1200 CHF for the full three-day course. However, GCSP encourages applications from women based in Geneva who come from conflict-affected or fragile regions and are unable to pay the tuition. These candidates may apply for a tuition waiver, ensuring that financial constraints do not prevent deserving women from participating.

GCSP reserves the right to accept or decline applications at its discretion, reflecting the selective and purpose-driven nature of the programme.

Why this programme matters

Women’s leadership in peace and security is essential for sustainable solutions, inclusive institutions and conflict prevention efforts worldwide. Despite their crucial roles, women remain underrepresented in decision-making spaces within diplomacy, conflict resolution and global security. Initiatives like the GCSP programme help fill this gap—by equipping women with the skills, confidence and networks they need to lead with impact.

Participants not only gain new competencies, but also join a global community of leaders who share a commitment to advancing peace, stability and gender-inclusive security.

Key details at a glance

  • Programme dates: 30 March – 1 April 2026
  • Location: Geneva, Switzerland
  • Application deadline: 15 March 2026
  • Duration: 3 days
  • Fee: 1200 CHF (tuition waiver available for eligible applicants)

With applications now open, mid-career women in the peace and security sector have an opportunity to invest in their growth, refine their leadership voice and join a community dedicated to shaping a more secure and inclusive world.

Application can be accessed here.

To know more about such opportunities, click here.

The post Women’s Leadership in Peace and Security 2026: Applications Open appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Rosa Trustee Application Pack: How to Apply for Trustee Roles at the UK Fund for Women and Girls (2026)
item.dc:creator: Susan Kamene
item.description:
Are you passionate about women’s rights, equality and social justice in the UK? Do you want to influence strategic decisions, governance, and funding that support women-led organisations across the country? Rosa UK Fund for Women and Girls is recruiting new Trustees – and this is your definitive guide to applying! Why Become a Trustee at […]

The post Rosa Trustee Application Pack: How to Apply for Trustee Roles at the UK Fund for Women and Girls (2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about women’s rights, equality and social justice in the UK? Do you want to influence strategic decisions, governance, and funding that support women-led organisations across the country? Rosa UK Fund for Women and Girls is recruiting new Trustees – and this is your definitive guide to applying!

Why Become a Trustee at Rosa?

Becoming a Trustee at Rosa puts you at the heart of an organisation dedicated to building a thriving, resilient women and girls sector – especially organisations that are often underfunded and overlooked.

As a Trustee you will:

  • Help set strategic direction and purpose for the charity.

  • Ensure legal, financial and governance compliance.

  • Support senior staff and connect Rosa to broader networks.

  • Champion causes that improve women’s lives across the UK.

This isn’t just governance – it’s impactful leadership in the women’s sector.

Who Can Apply – Qualifications & Eligibility

While Trustees are volunteers, Rosa is looking for candidates who:

  • Share a commitment to women and girls’ equality.

  • Can bring skills in grant-making, fundraising, communications, financial governance or strategy.

  • May have private sector experience or governance knowledge.

  • Can contribute to an inclusive and diverse board – especially people from underrepresented backgrounds.

No previous Trustee experience is required – Rosa welcomes new leaders who are committed and strategic.

Trustee Responsibilities – What You’ll Do

Trustees help ensure Rosa is well-run and impactful. Typical responsibilities include:

1. Strategic & Governance Leadership

  • Provide oversight of organisational strategy and performance.

  • Ensure compliance with charity law and regulatory standards.

  • Review and refine policies that protect beneficiaries and staff.

2. Operational Support

  • Participate in quarterly board meetings and sub-committee work.

  • Act as ambassadors for Rosa in your professional and community networks.

  • Offer guidance on fundraising, communications, financial oversight or programme evaluation.

3. Governance Culture

  • Uphold values of integrity, independence and accountability.

  • Support a transparent and inclusive board culture.

Benefits of Serving as a Rosa Trustee

Being a Trustee is voluntary and unpaid, but comes with rich non-financial benefits:

1. Professional Growth

  • Deep governance and leadership experience

  • Exposure to high-level strategy and nonprofit funding dynamics

2. Network Expansion

  • Connections in the women’s sector, philanthropy, public policy and civil society

  • Opportunity to work with senior leaders and influencers

3. Personal Impact

  • Contribution to systemic change for women and girls across the UK

  • Meaningful role in shaping funding, policy and access to services

How to Apply – Step-by-Step

1.Download & Review the Trustee Info Pack

Visit Rosa’s website trustee page for a full application pack, role description and governance documents:
Click here

2. Prepare Your Application

Applications typically require:

  • A CV or résumé highlighting relevant experience

  • A cover letter explaining:

    • Why you want to be a Rosa Trustee

    • What skills you bring to the board

  • Answers to any specific questions in the application form

3. Submit Before Deadline

 Complete the online application form via Rosa’s website before 2nd April 2026.

Key Deadlines (2026 Trustee Recruitment Timeline)

The recruitment timetable from Rosa’s Trustee recruitment listing includes:

Milestone Date
Application Deadline 5pm, Thursday 2nd April 2026
First Interviews (Remote) 29th & 30th April 2026
Second Interviews (In-Person in London) 12th May 2026
Board Strategy Day / First Meeting 30th June 2026
All applicants must be UK-based.

Final Tips for a Winning Application

• Align your cover letter with Rosa’s mission and values.
• Highlight governance, leadership or sector experience.
• Be clear about your motivations for serving as Trustee.
• Reach out for an informal chat before applying to clarify expectations.

Joining the Board of Trustees at Rosa is a rare opportunity to lend your skills and voice to an organisation at the forefront of championing women’s rights and equality in the UK.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Rosa Trustee Application Pack: How to Apply for Trustee Roles at the UK Fund for Women and Girls (2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
